appraisal_events.evidence: « adhéra entièrement »; « je sentais la bonne influence de sa maîtresse »; Robert rassure: « Tranquillise-toi, Zézette, il reviendra, il sera acquitté ».
appraisal_events.explanation: The narrator frames Robert favorably: he approves of the accusations deemed just against his family and shows tact and compassion in comforting. Despite the reservation about Champagne, the dominant tone elevates him.

status_effects.explanation: In this passage, Robert appears better judged: he follows a supposed good influence and adopts a consoling and assured posture.
